Course Title: Cybersecurity Risks in Public Sector AI Systems
Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Government employees, public sector administrators, civil servants, local government staff, educational administrators, school district personnel, non-profit managers, public service coordinators, municipal workers, city planners, social services staff
Team or Dept this is for: Government Operations, Public Administration, IT, Information Security, Risk Management, Compliance, Legal, Education Administration, Human Services, Public Safety
Description: Protecting critical services from disruption and misuse.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with everyday digital tools and a general awareness of how public sector organisations use technology is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the most common cybersecurity risks associated with AI systems used in public sector organisations and explain how these vulnerabilities can disrupt critical government and educational services. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise, report, and help mitigate threats, ensuring that AI-powered public services remain secure, trustworthy, and resilient against misuse.
